summary: Hezbollah continues to use fiber-optic FPV drones to strike Israeli armored vehicles, including two Namer MBTs and a HMMWV that caught fire. Russian operators at the Rubicon Center achieved a record 3,170 enemy target strikes in March 2026, a 40% increase over the previous record, with over 1,500 strikes on exposed infantry in the past six months. The Russian "Yolka" air defense drone demonstrated kinetic interceptions of enemy UAVs at distances over 1.5 km, causing warhead detonations. Meanwhile, Russian forces are recruiting professional instructors to train military personnel in operating multirotors, FPV drones, interceptors, and EOD/RCIED systems. In a separate incident, Ukrainian forces survived an FPV drone attack near Kostiantynivka, with footage showing a drone detector alerting them to the threat.
